user_message: Working on Blue Yonder Airlines's migration architecture. Got the escalation email -- help me investigate the decline.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-21 15:23 EST
Subject: URGENT: migration architecture Issue at Blue Yonder Airlines

There's been a change in our timeline for migration architecture. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 09:45 EST
Subject: Re: migration architecture Discussion - Next Steps

Our team encountered some challenges with migration architecture. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Robert Singh <robert.singh@blueyonderai.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-08 15:58 EST
Subject: Follow-up: migration architecture Review

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about migration architecture. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Blue Yonder Airlines: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-A trap (Treats casual workload mention as commitment); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-A failure mode (Treats casual workload mention as commitment)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-A -- Treats casual workload mention as commitment
